   174→
   175→好问题！让我看看你的 compaction（压缩）配置。
   176→
   177→
   178→> 💻 Check compaction config
   179→> ```
   180→> cat /Users/allenbot/openclaw/openclaw.json | grep -A 20 '"compaction"'
   181→> ```
   182→
   183→找到问题了！
   184→
   185→你的配置是 `"compaction": { "mode": "safeguard" }`
   186→
   187→**这意味着：**
   188→- `safeguard` 只是**保护**不崩溃，不会自动压缩
   189→- 不会把旧记忆写入文件释放 token
   190→- token 一直累积，直到爆炸
   191→
   192→**正确应该用 `memoryFlush` 模式** - 才会自动压缩：
   193→
   194→```json
   195→"compaction": {
   196→  "mode": "memoryFlush",
   197→  "reserveTokensFloor": 20000,
   198→  "memoryFlush": {
   199→    "enabled": true,
   200→    "softThresholdTokens": 4000,  // 剩 4000 token 时开始压缩
   201→    "systemPrompt": "Session nearing compaction...",
   202→    "prompt": "Write lasting notes to memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md; reply with NO_REPLY if nothing."
   203→  }
   204→}
   205→```
   206→
   207→**这就是"越来越笨"的原因：**
   208→1. 上下文太满 → 模型看到一堆无用历史
   209→2. 有效记忆被稀释 → 关键信息找不到
   210→3. 思考空间不足 → 简单任务也做不好
   211→4. 可能还在调用工具 → 恶性循环
   212→
   213→要我帮你改成 `memoryFlush` 模式吗？

  1576→# Browser Automation with agent-browser
  1577→
  1578→## Core Workflow
  1579→
  1580→Every browser automation follows this pattern:
  1581→
  1582→1. **Navigate**: `agent-browser open <url>`
  1583→2. **Snapshot**: `agent-browser snapshot -i` (get element refs like `@e1`, `@e2`)
  1584→3. **Interact**: Use refs to click, fill, select
  1585→4. **Re-snapshot**: After navigation or DOM changes, get fresh refs
  1586→
  1587→```bash
  1588→agent-browser open https://example.com/form
  1589→agent-browser snapshot -i
  1590→# Output: @e1 [input type="email"], @e2 [input type="password"], @e3 [button] "Submit"
  1591→
  1592→agent-browser fill @e1 "user@example.com"
  1593→agent-browser fill @e2 "password123"
  1594→agent-browser click @e3
  1595→agent-browser wait --load networkidle
  1596→agent-browser snapshot -i  # Check result
  1597→```
  1598→
  1599→## Command Chaining
  1600→
  1601→Commands can be chained with `&&` in a single shell invocation. The browser persists between commands via a background daemon, so chaining is safe and more efficient than separate calls.
  1602→
  1603→```bash
  1604→# Chain open + wait + snapshot in one call
  1605→agent-browser open https://example.com && agent-browser wait --load networkidle && agent-browser snapshot -i
  1606→
  1607→# Chain multiple interactions
  1608→agent-browser fill @e1 "user@example.com" && agent-browser fill @e2 "password123" && agent-browser click @e3
  1609→
  1610→# Navigate and capture
  1611→agent-browser open https://example.com && agent-browser wait --load networkidle && agent-browser screenshot page.png
  1612→```
  1613→
  1614→**When to chain:** Use `&&` when you don't need to read the output of an intermediate command before proceeding (e.g., open + wait + screenshot). Run commands separately when you need to parse the output first (e.g., snapshot to discover refs, then interact using those refs).
  1615→
  1616→## Essential Commands
  1617→
  1618→```bash
  1619→# Navigation
  1620→agent-browser open <url>              # Navigate (aliases: goto, navigate)
  1621→agent-browser close                   # Close browser
  1622→
  1623→# Snapshot
  1624→agent-browser snapshot -i             # Interactive elements with refs (recommended)
  1625→agent-browser snapshot -i -C          # Include cursor-interactive elements (divs with onclick, cursor:pointer)
  1626→agent-browser snapshot -s "#selector" # Scope to CSS selector
  1627→
  1628→# Interaction (use @refs from snapshot)
  1629→agent-browser click @e1               # Click element
  1630→agent-browser click @e1 --new-tab     # Click and open in new tab
  1631→agent-browser fill @e2 "text"         # Clear and type text
  1632→agent-browser type @e2 "text"         # Type without clearing
  1633→agent-browser select @e1 "option"     # Select dropdown option
  1634→agent-browser check @e1               # Check checkbox
  1635→agent-browser press Enter             # Press key
  1636→agent-browser scroll down 500         # Scroll page
  1637→
  1638→# Get information
  1639→agent-browser get text @e1            # Get element text
  1640→agent-browser get url                 # Get current URL
  1641→agent-browser get title               # Get page title
  1642→
  1643→# Wait
  1644→agent-browser wait @e1                # Wait for element
  1645→agent-browser wait --load networkidle # Wait for network idle
  1646→agent-browser wait --url "**/page"    # Wait for URL pattern
  1647→agent-browser wait 2000               # Wait milliseconds
  1648→
  1649→# Capture
  1650→agent-browser screenshot              # Screenshot to temp dir
  1651→agent-browser screenshot --full       # Full page screenshot
  1652→agent-browser screenshot --annotate   # Annotated screenshot with numbered element labels
  1653→agent-browser pdf output.pdf          # Save as PDF
  1654→```
  1655→
  1656→## Common Patterns
  1657→
  1658→### Form Submission
  1659→
  1660→```bash
  1661→agent-browser open https://example.com/signup
  1662→agent-browser snapshot -i
  1663→agent-browser fill @e1 "Jane Doe"
  1664→agent-browser fill @e2 "jane@example.com"
  1665→agent-browser select @e3 "California"
  1666→agent-browser check @e4
  1667→agent-browser click @e5
  1668→agent-browser wait --load networkidle
  1669→```
  1670→
  1671→### Authentication with State Persistence
  1672→
  1673→```bash
  1674→# Login once and save state
  1675→agent-browser open https://app.example.com/login
  1676→agent-browser snapshot -i
  1677→agent-browser fill @e1 "$USERNAME"
  1678→agent-browser fill @e2 "$PASSWORD"
  1679→agent-browser click @e3
  1680→agent-browser wait --url "**/dashboard"
  1681→agent-browser state save auth.json
  1682→
  1683→# Reuse in future sessions
  1684→agent-browser state load auth.json
  1685→agent-browser open https://app.example.com/dashboard
  1686→```
  1687→
  1688→### Session Persistence
  1689→
  1690→```bash
  1691→# Auto-save/restore cookies and localStorage across browser restarts
  1692→agent-browser --session-name myapp open https://app.example.com/login
  1693→# ... login flow ...
  1694→agent-browser close  # State auto-saved to ~/.agent-browser/sessions/
  1695→
  1696→# Next time, state is auto-loaded
  1697→agent-browser --session-name myapp open https://app.example.com/dashboard
  1698→
  1699→# Encrypt state at rest
  1700→export AGENT_BROWSER_ENCRYPTION_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
  1701→agent-browser --session-name secure open https://app.example.com
  1702→
  1703→# Manage saved states
  1704→agent-browser state list
  1705→agent-browser state show myapp-default.json
  1706→agent-browser state clear myapp
  1707→agent-browser state clean --older-than 7
  1708→```
  1709→
  1710→### Data Extraction
  1711→
  1712→```bash
  1713→agent-browser open https://example.com/products
  1714→agent-browser snapshot -i
  1715→agent-browser get text @e5           # Get specific element text
  1716→agent-browser get text body > page.txt  # Get all page text
  1717→
  1718→# JSON output for parsing
  1719→agent-browser snapshot -i --json
  1720→agent-browser get text @e1 --json
  1721→```
  1722→
  1723→### Parallel Sessions
  1724→
  1725→```bash
  1726→agent-browser --session site1 open https://site-a.com
  1727→agent-browser --session site2 open https://site-b.com
  1728→
  1729→agent-browser --session site1 snapshot -i
  1730→agent-browser --session site2 snapshot -i
  1731→
  1732→agent-browser session list
  1733→```
  1734→
  1735→### Connect to Existing Chrome
  1736→
  1737→```bash
  1738→# Auto-discover running Chrome with remote debugging enabled
  1739→agent-browser --auto-connect open https://example.com
  1740→agent-browser --auto-connect snapshot
  1741→
  1742→# Or with explicit CDP port
  1743→agent-browser --cdp 9222 snapshot
  1744→```
  1745→
  1746→### Visual Browser (Debugging)
  1747→
  1748→```bash
  1749→agent-browser --headed open https://example.com
  1750→agent-browser highlight @e1          # Highlight element
  1751→agent-browser record start demo.webm # Record session
  1752→agent-browser profiler start         # Start Chrome DevTools profiling
  1753→agent-browser profiler stop trace.json # Stop and save profile (path optional)
  1754→```
  1755→
  1756→### Local Files (PDFs, HTML)
  1757→
  1758→```bash
  1759→# Open local files with file:// URLs
  1760→agent-browser --allow-file-access open file:///path/to/document.pdf
  1761→agent-browser --allow-file-access open file:///path/to/page.html
  1762→agent-browser screenshot output.png
  1763→```
  1764→
  1765→### iOS Simulator (Mobile Safari)
  1766→
  1767→```bash
  1768→# List available iOS simulators
  1769→agent-browser device list
  1770→
  1771→# Launch Safari on a specific device
  1772→agent-browser -p ios --device "iPhone 16 Pro" open https://example.com
  1773→
  1774→# Same workflow as desktop - snapshot, interact, re-snapshot
  1775→agent-browser -p ios snapshot -i
  1776→agent-browser -p ios tap @e1          # Tap (alias for click)
  1777→agent-browser -p ios fill @e2 "text"
  1778→agent-browser -p ios swipe up         # Mobile-specific gesture
  1779→
  1780→# Take screenshot
  1781→agent-browser -p ios screenshot mobile.png
  1782→
  1783→# Close session (shuts down simulator)
  1784→agent-browser -p ios close
  1785→```
  1786→
  1787→**Requirements:** macOS with Xcode, Appium (`npm install -g appium && appium driver install xcuitest`)
  1788→
  1789→**Real devices:** Works with physical iOS devices if pre-configured. Use `--device "<UDID>"` where UDID is from `xcrun xctrace list devices`.
  1790→
  1791→## Timeouts and Slow Pages
  1792→
  1793→The default Playwright timeout is 60 seconds for local browsers. For slow websites or large pages, use explicit waits instead of relying on the default timeout:
  1794→
  1795→```bash
  1796→# Wait for network activity to settle (best for slow pages)
  1797→agent-browser wait --load networkidle
  1798→
  1799→# Wait for a specific element to appear
  1800→agent-browser wait "#content"
  1801→agent-browser wait @e1
  1802→
  1803→# Wait for a specific URL pattern (useful after redirects)
  1804→agent-browser wait --url "**/dashboard"
  1805→
  1806→# Wait for a JavaScript condition
  1807→agent-browser wait --fn "document.readyState === 'complete'"
  1808→
  1809→# Wait a fixed duration (milliseconds) as a last resort
  1810→agent-browser wait 5000
  1811→```
  1812→
  1813→When dealing with consistently slow websites, use `wait --load networkidle` after `open` to ensure the page is fully loaded before taking a snapshot. If a specific element is slow to render, wait for it directly with `wait <selector>` or `wait @ref`.
  1814→
  1815→## Session Management and Cleanup
  1816→
  1817→When running multiple agents or automations concurrently, always use named sessions to avoid conflicts:
  1818→
  1819→```bash
  1820→# Each agent gets its own isolated session
  1821→agent-browser --session agent1 open site-a.com
  1822→agent-browser --session agent2 open site-b.com
  1823→
  1824→# Check active sessions
  1825→agent-browser session list
  1826→```
  1827→
  1828→Always close your browser session when done to avoid leaked processes:
  1829→
  1830→```bash
  1831→agent-browser close                    # Close default session
  1832→agent-browser --session agent1 close   # Close specific session
  1833→```
  1834→
  1835→If a previous session was not closed properly, the daemon may still be running. Use `agent-browser close` to clean it up before starting new work.
  1836→
  1837→## Ref Lifecycle (Important)
  1838→
  1839→Refs (`@e1`, `@e2`, etc.) are invalidated when the page changes. Always re-snapshot after:
  1840→
  1841→- Clicking links or buttons that navigate
  1842→- Form submissions
  1843→- Dynamic content loading (dropdowns, modals)
  1844→
  1845→```bash
  1846→agent-browser click @e5              # Navigates to new page
  1847→agent-browser snapshot -i            # MUST re-snapshot
  1848→agent-browser click @e1              # Use new refs
  1849→```
  1850→
  1851→## Annotated Screenshots (Vision Mode)
  1852→
  1853→Use `--annotate` to take a screenshot with numbered labels overlaid on interactive elements. Each label `[N]` maps to ref `@eN`. This also caches refs, so you can interact with elements immediately without a separate snapshot.
  1854→
  1855→```bash
  1856→agent-browser screenshot --annotate
  1857→# Output includes the image path and a legend:
  1858→#   [1] @e1 button "Submit"
  1859→#   [2] @e2 link "Home"
  1860→#   [3] @e3 textbox "Email"
  1861→agent-browser click @e2              # Click using ref from annotated screenshot
  1862→```
  1863→
  1864→Use annotated screenshots when:
  1865→- The page has unlabeled icon buttons or visual-only elements
  1866→- You need to verify visual layout or styling
  1867→- Canvas or chart elements are present (invisible to text snapshots)
  1868→- You need spatial reasoning about element positions
  1869→
  1870→## Semantic Locators (Alternative to Refs)
  1871→
  1872→When refs are unavailable or unreliable, use semantic locators:
  1873→
  1874→```bash
  1875→agent-browser find text "Sign In" click
  1876→agent-browser find label "Email" fill "user@test.com"
  1877→agent-browser find role button click --name "Submit"
  1878→agent-browser find placeholder "Search" type "query"
  1879→agent-browser find testid "submit-btn" click
  1880→```
  1881→
  1882→## JavaScript Evaluation (eval)
  1883→
  1884→Use `eval` to run JavaScript in the browser context. **Shell quoting can corrupt complex expressions** -- use `--stdin` or `-b` to avoid issues.
  1885→
  1886→```bash
  1887→# Simple expressions work with regular quoting
  1888→agent-browser eval 'document.title'
  1889→agent-browser eval 'document.querySelectorAll("img").length'
  1890→
  1891→# Complex JS: use --stdin with heredoc (RECOMMENDED)
  1892→agent-browser eval --stdin <<'EVALEOF'
  1893→JSON.stringify(
  1894→  Array.from(document.querySelectorAll("img"))
  1895→    .filter(i => !i.alt)
  1896→    .map(i => ({ src: i.src.split("/").pop(), width: i.width }))
  1897→)
  1898→EVALEOF
  1899→
  1900→# Alternative: base64 encoding (avoids all shell escaping issues)
  1901→agent-browser eval -b "$(echo -n 'Array.from(document.querySelectorAll("a")).map(a => a.href)' | base64)"
  1902→```
  1903→
  1904→**Why this matters:** When the shell processes your command, inner double quotes, `!` characters (history expansion), backticks, and `$()` can all corrupt the JavaScript before it reaches agent-browser. The `--stdin` and `-b` flags bypass shell interpretation entirely.
  1905→
  1906→**Rules of thumb:**
  1907→- Single-line, no nested quotes -> regular `eval 'expression'` with single quotes is fine
  1908→- Nested quotes, arrow functions, template literals, or multiline -> use `eval --stdin <<'EVALEOF'`
  1909→- Programmatic/generated scripts -> use `eval -b` with base64
  1910→
  1911→## Configuration File
  1912→
  1913→Create `agent-browser.json` in the project root for persistent settings:
  1914→
  1915→```json
  1916→{
  1917→  "headed": true,
  1918→  "proxy": "http://localhost:8080",
  1919→  "profile": "./browser-data"
  1920→}
  1921→```
  1922→
  1923→Priority (lowest to highest): `~/.agent-browser/config.json` < `./agent-browser.json` < env vars < CLI flags. Use `--config <path>` or `AGENT_BROWSER_CONFIG` env var for a custom config file (exits with error if missing/invalid). All CLI options map to camelCase keys (e.g., `--executable-path` -> `"executablePath"`). Boolean flags accept `true`/`false` values (e.g., `--headed false` overrides config). Extensions from user and project configs are merged, not replaced.

现在我已经读完了整个会话。下面是摘要：

---

## 会话摘要（S0: e5cd5291, 02-21, 134条消息）

**讨论了什么：**
- OpenClaw agents "越来越笨"的问题排查：发现会话 token 超限（235k/200k = 117%），多个会话接近上限
- **compaction（压缩）配置排查**：发现 `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`（系统配置）用的是 `safeguard` 模式（不会自动压缩），而 `~/openclaw/openclaw.json`（备份）有 `memoryFlush` 配置但没生效
- 尝试把 `safeguard` 改成 `memoryFlush`，发现 OpenClaw 不认这个值，改回去
- 尝试从文档找到正确的 compaction 配置（`enabled: true, reserveTokens, keepRecentTokens`），但 `openclaw config validate` 也不认，再次改回 safeguard
- 讨论 BotFather/Telegram 设置是否导致问题（Privacy Mode = DISABLED），但用户只有自己一个人，排除此因素
- 发现 `/compact` 命令报错：`"nomic-embed-text" does not support chat` — **压缩时错误地使用了嵌入模型（nomic-embed-text）而非对话模型（MiniMax-M2.5）**
- 讨论 ollama vs node-llama-cpp vs 本地 GGUF 的关系：memorySearch 用的是本地 GGUF 文件，不依赖 ollama
- 用户确认 `ollama launch claude` 是用来启动 Claude Code 的，所以 ollama 本身不能删
- 最终决定：从 OpenClaw 配置中删除 ollama provider，只保留 MiniMax-M2.5，用 API Key 直连（替代 OAuth）
- 删除旧配置并运行 `openclaw configure` 重新配置
- 用户两次提供了 MiniMax API Key（sk-cp-AVjcKaaz...）

**改了什么代码：**
- `/Users/allenbot/.openclaw/openclaw.json` — 多次修改：
  - compaction mode: safeguard -> memoryFlush -> 改回 safeguard
  - 尝试添加顶层 compaction 配置 -> 不支持 -> 删除
  - primary 模型: `ollama/nomic-embed-text` -> `minimax-portal/MiniMax-M2.5`
  - 删除 ollama provider 配置
  - 从 OAuth 认证改为 API Key 直连
  - 最终删除配置文件重新配置（`openclaw configure`）
- `/Users/allenbot/.openclaw/credentials/` — 修复目录权限从 755 改为 700
- 超限会话文件 `~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/ef431da7-...-topic-6091.jsonl`（3.1MB）由用户手动移走

**关键决策：**
- **根因未完全解决**：`/compact` 命令用了嵌入模型（nomic-embed-text）而非对话模型，这是 OpenClaw 的 bug 或配置问题。删除 ollama provider + 重新配置可能解决此问题，但会话结束时尚未验证 `/compact` 是否恢复正常
- **保留 ollama 本体**（因为 `ollama launch claude` 需要它），只删除 OpenClaw 配置中的 ollama provider
- **从 OAuth 改为 API Key 直连**
- **配置文件最终选择重新生成**（删除旧配置 + `openclaw configure`），而非继续修补
